purpose: The commit fixes a bug in the Bluetooth stack by preventing queuing commands when HCI_UNREGISTER is set.
The commit removes an unnecessary include directive in a source file.
The commit updates documentation for a new metric added to the store operations.
The commit fixes a bug in the hugetlb vma lock handling during split vma and range unmapping.
The commit adds two new supplies (AVDD and MICVDD) to an existing codec binding for a Realtek audio codec.
The commit fixes a resource leak in the remove function of the zorro7xx driver.
The commit removes an unused field from a struct in the net/mlx5e module.
The commit changes the type of a struct member from unsigned to signed, which is a minor technical change that does not affect the functionality of the code.
The commit sorts CONFIG_SURFACE_PLATFORMS in defconfigs for RISC-V, which is a configuration option for the Linux kernel.
The commit introduces a new function to check if the chip's FIFOs are configured for CAN-FD mode.
The commit adds a new test for the RTC driver that skips when the RTC is not present.
The commit removes a redundant assignment to a variable called 'bytes' in the echoaudio driver.
The commit decouples the presence of rcuscale from the presence of RCU Tasks in a CONFIG_PREEMPT_NONE=y kernel.
The commit removes duplicated text in a header file for KVM's MMU implementation.
The commit reverts a change that populates a clock table based on DCFCLK states, which causes an underflow issue.
The commit adds a new compatible string for the Renesas RZ/V2L SoC's Bifrost Mali-G31 GPU.
The commit renames a struct in the DW PCIe core driver to make it more consistent with other names.
The commit fixes a warning in the clang compiler by using the correct format specifier for an unsigned short value.
The commit fixes a bug in the MIPS jump_label function by casting the upper bound of the branch range to long to do a signed compare and avoid negative offsets triggering a warning.
The commit removes a case from SetHwReg8188EU() that calls a function. This is part of the ongoing effort to get rid of the unwanted hal layer.
The commit declares non-global symbols as static to fix sparse warnings introduced while adding errata.
The commit removes incorrect interrupt flags from display/msm nodes.
The commit is allocating arrays in a struct for future use.
The commit adds new SoCs and PMICs to the socinfo driver, which is responsible for providing information about the system-on-chip (SoC) and power management integrated circuit (PMIC).
The commit updates the chip cut recognition in the rtl8xxxu driver to include all possible values instead of just up to E.
The commit drops a custom software enable count for PRCMU clocks in the core.
The commit is a refactoring of the mm/init code to remove #ifdef CONFIG_XIP_KERNEL usage.
The commit enables QUP and GPI DMA hardware to support I2C nodes.
The commit fixes a typo in a comment and improves code style by moving a comment to its own line.
The commit fixes an infinite loop that occurs when releasing software-based flow synchronization resources.
The commit updates the pcm3168a driver to use modern terminology for clocking.
The commit removes a hack that was used to check if a file is a swapfile.
The commit adds a new feature to the GTP device driver by implementing the echo response functionality.
The commit replaces cpumask_next_* functions with cpumask_first_* where appropriate. It also creates a new function called cpumask_first_zero().
The commit removes a call to an IOVAR that is no longer supported in the firmware, which would cause a failure message to be displayed on the console.
The commit adds support for a new platform without an amplifier by connecting the alc5682 headset codec to SSP0.
The commit modifies the behavior of zerocopy send requests for protocols that don't support it.
The commit adds a test to ensure that xeCJK.sty is loaded only if it exists.
The commit modifies the scripts/checkpatch.pl script to allow macros with complex values enclosed in parentheses.
The commit replaces simple-KMS helpers with regular atomic helpers for better code sharing among drivers.
The commit adds a new interface for jpeg decoding workers to ensure parallel execution of three HWs in MT8195.
The commit removes unnecessary VPRINTK() calls and a function that is no longer needed.
The commit adds support for zstate during extended vblank, which is a feature that allows the display to enter a low-power state when not displaying content.
The commit fixes a bug in the kernel's Control Flow Integrity (CFI) feature by updating the function pointer prototype to match reality.
The commit fixes a bug in the pinctrl driver by correcting the name of a property from 'reset' to 'resets'. The change also adds missing 'additionalProperties: false'.
The commit is a refactoring change that silences an array-bounds warning in GCC 12.
The commit changes the way the system handles runtime power management for the sun8i-ss device.
The commit splits out fixed file installation and removal from io_uring and moves it to a separate file called filetable. It also takes a ctx rather than an io_kiocb.
The commit splits the G1 and G2 decoder blocks into separate nodes in the device tree, allowing for independent power management and enabling the vpu-blk-ctrl power domain controller.
The commit removes a macro that was only used once in the codebase.
